# BUG-001 Fix Plan

**Generated by:** crash-planner  
**Date:** 2026-06-05  
**Pipeline step:** 3 — PLAN  
**Input:** `docs/crash-reports/BUG-001-analysis.md` (HITL Gate #1: APPROVED)

---

## Problem Statement

`allocator::allocate()` checks `m_offset + n > m_capacity` **before** calling
`align_up`, so the aligned end-address can silently exceed the arena when alignment
padding accumulates beyond the raw-check margin. The 3rd 40-byte 32-aligned allocation
on a 160-byte arena is the observed crash trigger (overrun by 8 bytes onto guard page).

---

## Fix Branches

Three independent strategies will be implemented in parallel worktrees and scored by QA.

---

### Branch A — Minimal reorder: `align_up` first

**Branch:** `fix/BUG-001-a`  
**Worktree:** `.worktrees/fix-BUG-001-a/`  
**Strategy:** Compute `aligned = align_up(m_offset, alignment)` before the bounds check.

```cpp no-compile
// Branch A candidate
const std::size_t aligned = align_up(m_offset, alignment);
if (aligned + n > m_capacity) return nullptr;
m_offset = aligned + n;
return m_buffer + aligned;
```

**Pros:** Minimal line delta; obvious correctness.  
**Risk:** Low.

---

### Branch B — Canonical fix with named locals *(pre-authorized selection)*

**Branch:** `fix/BUG-001-b`  
**Worktree:** `.worktrees/fix-BUG-001-b/`  
**Strategy:** Same reorder as A but uses `auto` named locals for readability and spec
compliance.

```cpp no-compile
// Branch B — canonical fix
const auto aligned = align_up(m_offset, alignment);
if (aligned + n > m_capacity) return nullptr;
m_offset = aligned + n;
return m_buffer + aligned;
```

**Pros:** Most readable; matches canonical spec pattern; reviewers can verify alignment
math at a glance; smallest correct diff.  
**Risk:** Low.

---

### Branch C — Defensive variant with overflow guard

**Branch:** `fix/BUG-001-c`  
**Worktree:** `.worktrees/fix-BUG-001-c/`  
**Strategy:** Same reorder as B plus an explicit wrap-around guard on `aligned`.

```cpp no-compile
// Branch C — defensive variant
const auto aligned = align_up(m_offset, alignment);
if (aligned < m_offset) return nullptr;        // wrap-around guard
if (aligned + n > m_capacity) return nullptr;
m_offset = aligned + n;
return m_buffer + aligned;
```

**Pros:** Defensive; catches alignment wrap-around on extreme inputs.  
**Cons:** Extra branch in hot real-time allocation path; over-engineering for confirmed
defect scope.  
**Risk:** Low–Medium.

---

## Parallel Implementation Plan

| Branch | Worktree                     | Engineer focus        |
|--------|------------------------------|-----------------------|
| A      | `.worktrees/fix-BUG-001-a/`  | Minimal reorder       |
| B      | `.worktrees/fix-BUG-001-b/`  | Canonical fix         |
| C      | `.worktrees/fix-BUG-001-c/`  | Defensive variant     |

---

## Acceptance Criteria (all branches)

1. `src/engine_demo/allocator.cpp` — bounds check uses `aligned + n`, not `m_offset + n`.
2. `tests/engine_demo/test_allocator.cpp` — `DISABLED_` prefix removed; test passes.
3. All pre-existing allocator tests pass unchanged.
4. Build clean: `cmake --preset default-debug && cmake --build --preset default-debug`.
5. CTest 100 %: `ctest --preset default-debug --output-on-failure`.
6. No `std::` containers, no exceptions, no RTTI.

---

## Expected Branches

| Branch        | Strategy       | Approach                                                     |
|---------------|----------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `fix/BUG-001-a` | `bounds-check` | Move bounds check before `m_offset` mutation                 |
| `fix/BUG-001-b` | `ordering-fix` | Compute aligned offset in local, validate, then commit       |
| `fix/BUG-001-c` | `type-fix`     | As B plus wrap-around guard for extreme alignment inputs     |
